items.quote: eInvoicing and CLM contract management confirmed out of scope on the call (DocuSign CLM handles it instead), but SF Products__c still lists eInvoicing.
Jim Boss and David Single are named Executive Sponsor/Evaluator contact roles but neither has appeared on any of the 12 calls read.
Harman Toor (Corporate Counsel) is a named contact role but has never appeared on a call; no MSA/SOW/legal timeline has surfaced.

metrics.narrative: Confirmed pain is operational, not yet fully quantified: Katie Booth and Yufeng Chen described 'an urgent need for an iFAX module and a supply information module, along with significant improvements in performance and dashboards, which have become increasingly difficult to manage' (Avoma call 10 Mar 2026). Zycus proved classification value live by processing S&C's Amazon spend data (4,400 lines) for accuracy during the 2 Feb 2026 AI session, but no buyer-stated dollar savings or cycle-time figure has been captured on any read call. Quantifying the business case (hours saved per RFx, error-rate reduction) is the open move.

champion.narrative: Yufeng Chen is a strong, active champion: on the 3 Jun 2026 call, after Zycus's rep raised the possibility of withdrawing over direct-material gaps, Yufeng pushed back directly -- 'we feel very strongly about Zycus... the AI is just amazing... we just don't think Oracle is gonna be able to meet even 50% of this stuff' -- and by 24 Jun 2026 confirmed Zycus as Vendor of Choice heading into the final demo. Katie Booth operates as an active operational co-champion, managing scoring mechanics, SME scheduling, and vendor-relationship continuity across every call from 30 Jan through 30 Jun 2026. Neither shows signs of cooling.

competition.narrative: Three-way field: Oracle (S&C's incumbent ERP, championed internally by CIO Bob King 'at IT's insistence' per Next_Step__c 24 Jun 2026, despite the buying team stating 'no one is happy with Oracle as a partner'), and iValua (retained only because it was shortlisted in a prior initiative, not on current merit). Zycus was confirmed Vendor of Choice on 24 Jun 2026, and the wedge is AI/Teams orchestration plus indirect eProcurement strength -- the buyer explicitly said 'we don't think anybody comes close to us at this time' referring to the relevant stakeholder capability (Avoma call 3 Jun 2026). The live threat is not a rival vendor's demo performance but CIO-level internal politics favoring Oracle.

identify_pain.narrative: Confirmed pain: 'there has been ongoing indecision and conflict regarding responses and actions, leading to a lack of conclusive decisions and ineffective communication with bidders' (Avoma call 14 Jan 2026), and later 'an urgent need for an iFAX module and a supply information module... performance and dashboards... increasingly difficult to manage' (Avoma call 10 Mar 2026). Yufeng Chen separately framed the urgency in digital-transformation terms: 's and see is so far behind on the digital transformation... what are we shuffling our feet for here?' (Avoma call 3 Jun 2026). Pain owner is the Global Sourcing/Supply Chain team (Yufeng Chen, Katie Booth).

sources: Avoma manifest (12 calls read, no legal contact present)
paper_process.narrative: No MSA/SOW/legal milestone has surfaced on any read call, and Harman Toor (Corporate Counsel, named OpportunityContactRole) has never appeared as a speaker. With final RFP submission not due until 18 Jul 2026 and the recorded close date at 24 Jul 2026, there is effectively no visible runway for contracting. Getting a documented paper-process plan (who drafts, whose paper, InfoSec timing) in front of Harman Toor now would create real visibility.

economic_buyer.narrative: Jim Boss (VP Global Operational Excellence) is the SF-recorded Executive Sponsor/Decision Maker and David Single (VP Finance) is a named Evaluator/Influencer, but neither has appeared as a speaker on any of the 12 calls read. The Next Step (24 Jun 2026) instead names 'the VP of Supply Chain' as the active executive sponsor pushing for a vendor-led implementation Oracle and iValua reportedly won't match -- consistent with the January 2026 call describing the initiative as 'driven by new VP of Supply Chain Kara Weiner.' Budget authority is therefore referenced but not directly engaged by Zycus on a call; direct EB contact is the priority gap.

decision_process.narrative: The call sequence itself maps the process: procurement (Dan Chao) drives demo logistics and ~35 SME scheduling; Katie Booth manages evaluation mechanics and vendor-relationship continuity; Yufeng Chen adjudicates scope and fit decisions in real time (including nearly withdrawing Zycus over a gap, then reversing course, on the 3 Jun 2026 call). An on-site executive review of demo outcomes was planned for early July 2026 (Avoma call 8 Jun 2026), and Bob King's IT deep-dive with the IT lead remains a pending, unresolved step per Next_Step__c (24 Jun 2026).

decision_criteria.narrative: S&C built a detailed 217-line RFP/SOW tabulated by functional category with a common scorecard evaluated across module functionality, strategic supplier capability/roadmap, UX, change management, AI strategy, and total cost (Avoma calls 30 Jan 2026 and 14 May 2026). Each evaluator scores line-by-line against out-of-box/configurable/custom classifications, and Zycus explicitly aligned its demo order to the buyer's scoring tabs (Avoma call 30 Jun 2026).

north_star_verdict.math: 16 days to close (today 2026-07-08 -> 2026-07-24). Deal has been in an RFP/demo cycle since a revised 217-requirement SOW went out 14-16 May 2026; final RFP submission was reset on 18 Jun 2026 to 18 Jul 2026, leaving only 6 days between requirements-lock and the recorded close date for demo scoring, executive review, pricing, and contracting -- none of which (beyond pricing) has visibly started. Realistic pace points to a close well beyond 24 Jul.
evidence: Next_Step__c (24 Jun 2026): 'Mtg with Katie to confirm Zycus is VOC going into final demo.'
Next_Step__c (18 Jun 2026): 'RH New timeline - final RFP submission with new requirements - 7/18/26.'
Avoma call 30 Jun 2026: formal priced RFP response still outstanding ('as soon as you can... need the dollar figures'); demo dates still being finalized for the week of 8-10 Jul or 13-17 Jul.
north_star_verdict.headline: Buyer named Zycus Vendor of Choice on 24 Jun 2026, but the 24 Jul close date is not credible: final RFP submission isn't due until 18 Jul, no MSA/SOW/legal work has started, and Corporate Counsel has never appeared on a call.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Push to Best Case with a realistic close in September 2026, allowing time after the 18 Jul RFP submission for demo scoring, executive review, pricing sign-off, and a paper process that has not yet begun.

flags: No quantified business case: qualitative pain (vendor-led implementation need, change-management constraint) is documented but no ROI, cost savings, cycle-time reduction, or headcount impact. Economic Buyer and Executive Sponsor have not articulated financial metrics. This weakens forecast defensibility and leaves the deal vulnerable if budget or priority shifts.

Paper-process timeline gap: 18 days to close, no documented MSA/SOW milestones, legal-review process, or InfoSec engagement despite Harman Toor (Corporate Counsel) in contact roles. Risk of contracting delay if not surfaced and planned in final demo conversation.

The direct-materials gap (PLM/3DX, VMI, OTM) is now openly disclosed in Zycus's own RFP response; use the 3 requested references (smooth/some-issues/problematic) to show how other manufacturing customers absorbed similar gaps, turning candor into a differentiator against Oracle and iValua.

Champion Katie Booth and coach Yufeng Chen are actively coaching Zycus on scoring mechanics and demo structure; convert that goodwill into a direct introduction to the relevant stakeholder Boss ahead of the early-July on-site executive review of demo outcomes.

Convert the verbal 'vendor of choice' signal into a written scoring understanding before demos start, protecting Zycus from being penalized on the 15-20% direct-materials deviation in the 217-line RFP.
The direct-materials gap is the only real technical objection; S&C's own champions said it won't sink the deal, so keep the app-based 3DX roadmap concrete and dated.

Lock in writing that direct materials won't be scored against Zycus for eProcurement, now that Yufeng has verbally confirmed it twice (24 and 30 Jun 2026), before the 35-evaluator scorecard is finalized.

Land the PLM/3DX wireframes and phased-development story convincingly at the final demo -- 15-20% of the 217 lines deviate, concentrated in eProcurement/direct materials, and the buyer has signaled this won't hurt scoring only if shown credibly.
best_practice_check.summary: The deal's momentum is real (VOC status, strong champion, active pricing/demo cadence) but three levers now determine whether it converts on time.

customer_preference.evidence: Yufeng Chen: 'we feel very strongly about Zycus... we don't think Oracle is gonna be able to meet even 50% of this stuff' (3 Jun 2026); Next Step confirms Zycus as Vendor of Choice heading into final demo (24 Jun 2026).

competitive_position.summary: Zycus is the buyer-stated Vendor of Choice as of 24 Jun 2026. Oracle is the highest current threat, not on demo merit but through Bob King's internal push ('at IT's insistence') despite the wider buying team's view that 'no one is happy with Oracle as a partner.' iValua is a low-threat holdover, retained only because it was shortlisted in a prior initiative. Coupa appears only in the SF Competitor field and in a tangential rep-relationship anecdote; no evidence surfaced this run of Coupa being actively evaluated against this opportunity.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Zycus is reading as the buyer's preferred vendor going into a final demo it hasn't yet completed, with real momentum from a compressed run of June calls, but the close date, contracting readiness, and a lingering IT/Oracle threat are well behind where a 15-day-out Upside/Key deal needs to be.

deal_risk.text: The recorded 24 Jul 2026 close sits only 15 days out, with no MSA/SOW/legal engagement visible and Corporate Counsel Harman Toor never appearing on a read call.
The CIO's Oracle preference is an unresolved wild card that Zycus's own Next Step flags as a risk despite the current vendor-of-choice positioning.
The final demo may not yet be complete, and the RFP resubmission isn't due until 18 Jul 2026, leaving very little runway to the 24 Jul 2026 close.

win_position.text: Katie Booth confirmed Zycus as vendor of choice heading into the final demo in a 24 Jun 2026 meeting, and iValua remains in the process only as a holdover from a prior shortlisted initiative, not a live threat.
The Executive Sponsor wants a vendor-led implementation approach that, per the rep's 2 Jul 2026 Next Step, neither Oracle nor iValua is willing to provide.
No economic buyer has engaged directly on pricing, ROI, or budget sign-off on any read call, and the CIO's continued push for Oracle remains an unresolved wild card that could surface after the demo.
Zycus itself estimates it can meet only 80-85% of the reworked 217-line RFP, with 5-10% not buildable at all, concentrated in direct-materials/PLM integration (24 Jun 2026 call).

deal_momentum.text: A two-way buyer email reply landed on 8 Jul 2026 (one day before this sweep), continuing a run of five buyer-facing calls through June covering scope, RFP gaps, and demo logistics.
The final-demo window is being locked for the week of 13-17 Jul 2026, with a roughly 35-person SME evaluator list being finalized (30 Jun 2026 call).

The original RFP-response/demo-evaluation window (22-30 Jun 2026) already slipped once into July, and the formal priced RFP response Zycus owes S&C's leadership had still not gone out as of this sweep.

customer_commitment.text: The buyer has built a 217-line RFP with a dedicated scorecard and ~35 SME evaluators, and continues to engage two-way, including the 8 Jul 2026 email reply.
Both Katie Booth and Yufeng Chen have pushed back on the idea of scoring Zycus down purely for direct-materials gaps, actively advocating to keep Zycus in the competition (24 Jun 2026 call).
Commitment so far is process-level (scoring, scheduling); no budget or ROI conversation has occurred with the Executive Sponsor or VP Finance on any read call.

hard.ais_why:
• On the 73-minute 2/2/2026 demo, Yufeng Chen drove the AI conversation himself. Direct quotes: "Is your AI be able to do this?", "Is that an executable task for the agents?", and "how much of your percentage of revenue spend on AI development?" The note tagged as key takeaway is that he wants deeper insights into underlying algorithms and control mechanisms for a follow-up detailed demo.
• Yufeng pushed on Anna autonomous negotiation algorithms and guardrails, specifically how style is controlled and corporate reputation maintained. He also committed to share 4,400 lines of Amazon Business transaction data under NDA so Zycus could process it within a week and demo classification accuracy. That is buyer-funded, buyer-initiated POC behavior.
• Concrete next steps reinforce hunger: Zycus to send a mapping of platform names to each requirement tab, an AI agent capabilities slide, iSave plus Anna negotiation agent details, and an ESG scope and timeline assessment. Yufeng is asking for AI capability mapping, controls, and ROI: classic AI Hungry champion behavior.
